info.title: acpx gateway
info.description: Full JSON-RPC method registry acpx-server dispatches across every transport it exposes (stdio, HTTP POST /rpc, WS GET /ws -- all three share one dispatch surface, see docs/schema/README.md). Covers both directions: `x-acpx-side: client-to-agent` methods a connecting client sends to acpx, and `x-acpx-side: agent-to-client` methods acpx itself calls back out to that same connected client (relaying a request acpx's own spawned backend made to acpx one hop further). Raw ACP method shapes are $ref'd directly from the upstream agent-client-protocol crate's own derived schema, never re-authored -- see components.schemas for the merged acpx-native + upstream-referenced type registry.

Request.description: A JSON-RPC 2.0 request or notification (notifications omit `id`).

RequestId.description: JSON-RPC ids are either a string or a number on the wire.

Response.description: A JSON-RPC 2.0 response: exactly one of `result`/`error` is present.

AcpxExt.description:
Sibling extension object carried by `session/new` only: `{"_acpx": {"profile": "..."}}`.
Chosen as a single namespaced key (see architecture doc) so it can be
trivially stripped and can't collide with any current/future ACP field.

NewSessionParams.description:
`session/new` request params. `_acpx` is additive and stripped by the
router prior to forwarding -- see `AcpxExt` above.

**`#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]` (added post-real-e2e-schema-
validation, `acpx-openrpc-schema` plan):** upstream's real
`NewSessionRequest` (agent-client-protocol-schema 1.4.0,
`src/v1/agent.rs`) carries this same container attribute, so
`mcp_servers` is `mcpServers` on the actual wire -- every real ACP
client (including this workspace's own `real_ambient_multi_agent_
test.rs`) sends `"mcpServers"`, never `"mcp_servers"`. Before this
fix, this struct's missing `rename_all` meant a real
`{"cwd":..., "mcpServers": [...]}` request silently deserialized
with `mcp_servers` defaulted to empty and the real array captured by
`rest` (the `#[serde(flatten)]` catch-all) instead -- functionally
harmless *only* because nothing ever read `NewSessionParams.
mcp_servers` at runtime (`router.rs` never uses this type at all,
operating on raw `serde_json::Value` directly instead -- see this
module's own doc comment on why `NewSessionParams` is schema/test
tooling, not the live producer), but it meant the generated schema
document was simply *wrong* about the real wire field name. Found by
running the schema pipeline's own real-backend e2e validation
(`acpx-proto::validate`) against this exact test's literal
`"mcpServers": []` params.

GatewaySessionId.description:
Gateway-issued session id returned from a hybrid `session/new` call.
Distinct type from the backend's own session id (see
`acpx-core::session_registry`) so the two are never accidentally mixed up
at a call site.

oneOf.description: Registry entry known, nothing fetched/verified yet.

Runtime present, adapter installed/resolvable (e.g. `node`+`npm` on
`PATH` for an npx-distributed entry, or a fetched binary present).

Installed, but no native session/credentials detected yet (distinct
from `Installed` per `05-open-risks.md`'s "native session by
default" note -- a third status, not a boolean).

The distribution method's runtime dependency is missing (e.g. no
`node`/`npm` for an npx-only entry).

AgentIdParams.description:
`agents/install` and `agents/status` params -- both are exactly
`{"id": "..."}"` in `router.rs`.

AgentInstallResult.description:
`agents/install` result envelope. `outcome` is `acpx_registry::
InstallOutcome`'s `{outcome:?}`-formatted debug string (router.rs's
own choice, not a typed enum on the wire) -- kept as a bare `String`
here rather than inventing a closed enum this schema would then have
to keep in lockstep with `acpx-registry`'s.

GatewaySessionListEntry.description:
One entry in `session/list`'s default (no-selector) branch --
distinct from, and much smaller than, real ACP's `ListSessionsResponse`
entries, since this branch answers "which gateway sessions does this
acpx process itself know about", not a specific backend's own session
history. See `router.rs`'s `"session/list"` arm doc comment for why
this method is a genuine dual-shape split, not a rename.

GatewaySessionListResult.description:
`session/list`'s default (no-selector) result envelope. When the
caller supplies a selector instead, `router.rs` proxies to the real
backend's `ListSessionsRequest`/`ListSessionsResponse`
(`dispatch_session_list_real`) -- the method registry
(`methods.rs`) records both possibilities as a `oneOf`, this type
covers only the gateway-native half.

McpServerEntry.description:
A centrally-registered MCP server entry (`mcp_servers/create|update|
list`). Deliberately opaque beyond the `name` key acpx actually reads
(`acpx-core/src/mcp_servers.rs`'s doc comment: "`acpx` never
interprets an MCP server entry's fields itself") -- this is an
intentional design decision restated in schema form, not a gap to
close further (see `00-goal.md`'s non-goals).

NameOnlyParams.description:
`profiles/delete` and `mcp_servers/delete` params -- both are exactly
`{"name": "..."}"` in `router.rs`.

NameOnlyResult.description:
`profiles/delete` and `mcp_servers/delete` result envelope -- both
are exactly `{"name": "...", "deleted": true}"` in `router.rs`.

PermissionPolicySchema.description:
Mirrors `acpx_core::profile::PermissionPolicy` -- see this module's
doc comment for why this is a mirror rather than a direct reference.

ProfileSchema.description:
Mirrors `acpx_core::profile::Profile`'s wire shape exactly (field
names, optionality, defaults) -- the type `profiles/create`,
`profiles/update`, and `profiles/list` (per-element) all serialize on
the wire, post `redact_launch_overrides` (which only ever replaces
`launch_overrides` *values* with the literal string
`"***redacted***"`, never changes the shape). `key_ref` mirrors
`acpx_core::keystore::KeyRef`'s newtype-transparent wire
representation (a bare string) as `Option<String>` directly rather
than introducing a single-field wrapper type purely for this file.

protocolVersion.description: The latest protocol version supported by the client.

clientInfo.description:
Information about the Client name and version sent to the Agent.

Note: in future versions of the protocol, this will be required.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

InitializeRequest.description:
Request parameters for the initialize method.

Sent by the client to establish connection and negotiate capabilities.

See protocol docs: [Initialization](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization)

ProtocolVersion.description:
Protocol version identifier.

This version is only bumped for breaking changes.
Non-breaking changes should be introduced via capabilities.

fs.description:
File system capabilities supported by the client.
Determines which file operations the agent can request.

terminal.description: Whether the Client support all `terminal/*` methods.

session.description:
Session-related capabilities supported by the client.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the client does not advertise any
session-related extensions.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

ClientCapabilities.description:
Capabilities supported by the client.

Advertised during initialization to inform the agent about
available features and methods.

See protocol docs: [Client Capabilities](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization#client-capabilities)

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

FileSystemCapabilities.description:
File system capabilities that a client may support.

See protocol docs: [FileSystem](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization#filesystem)

configOptions.description:
Config option capabilities supported by the client.

Omitted or `null` both mean the client does not advertise support for any
config option extensions.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

ClientSessionCapabilities.description: Session-related capabilities supported by the client.

boolean.description:
Whether the client supports boolean session configuration options.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the client does not advertise support.
Supplying `{}` means agents may include `type: "boolean"` entries in
`configOptions`, and the client may send `session/set_config_option`
requests with `type: "boolean"` and a boolean `value`.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionConfigOptionsCapabilities.description: Session configuration option capabilities supported by the client.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

BooleanConfigOptionCapabilities.description:
Capabilities for boolean session configuration options.

Supplying `{}` means the client supports boolean session configuration options.

name.description:
Intended for programmatic or logical use, but can be used as a display
name fallback if title isn’t present.

title.description:
Intended for UI and end-user contexts — optimized to be human-readable
and easily understood.

If not provided, the name should be used for display.

version.description:
Version of the implementation. Can be displayed to the user or used
for debugging or metrics purposes. (e.g. "1.0.0").

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

Implementation.description:
Metadata about the implementation of the client or agent.
Describes the name and version of an ACP implementation, with an optional
title for UI representation.

protocolVersion.description:
The protocol version the client specified if supported by the agent,
or the latest protocol version supported by the agent.

The client should disconnect, if it doesn't support this version.

authMethods.description: Authentication methods supported by the agent.

agentInfo.description:
Information about the Agent name and version sent to the Client.

Note: in future versions of the protocol, this will be required.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

InitializeResponse.description:
Response to the `initialize` method.

Contains the negotiated protocol version and agent capabilities.

See protocol docs: [Initialization](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization)

promptCapabilities.description: Prompt capabilities supported by the agent.

mcpCapabilities.description: MCP capabilities supported by the agent.

sessionCapabilities.description: Session lifecycle and prompt capabilities advertised by the agent.

auth.description: Authentication-related capabilities supported by the agent.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

AgentCapabilities.description:
Capabilities supported by the agent.

Advertised during initialization to inform the client about
available features and content types.

See protocol docs: [Agent Capabilities](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization#agent-capabilities)

embeddedContext.description:
Agent supports embedded context in `session/prompt` requests.

When enabled, the Client is allowed to include [`ContentBlock::Resource`]
in prompt requests for pieces of context that are referenced in the message.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

PromptCapabilities.description:
Prompt capabilities supported by the agent in `session/prompt` requests.

Baseline agent functionality requires support for [`ContentBlock::Text`]
and [`ContentBlock::ResourceLink`] in prompt requests.

Other variants must be explicitly opted in to.
Capabilities for different types of content in prompt requests.

Indicates which content types beyond the baseline (text and resource links)
the agent can process.

See protocol docs: [Prompt Capabilities](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization#prompt-capabilities)

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

McpCapabilities.description: MCP capabilities supported by the agent

list.description:
Whether the agent supports `session/list`.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the agent does not advertise support.
Supplying `{}` means the agent supports listing sessions.

delete.description:
Whether the agent supports `session/delete`.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the agent does not advertise support.
Supplying `{}` means the agent supports deleting sessions from `session/list`.

additionalDirectories.description:
Whether the agent supports `additionalDirectories` on supported session lifecycle requests.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the agent does not advertise support.
Supplying `{}` means the agent supports `additionalDirectories` on
supported session lifecycle requests.

Agents that also support `session/list` may return
`SessionInfo.additionalDirectories` to report the complete ordered
additional-root list associated with a listed session.

fork.description:
**UNSTABLE**

This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.

Whether the agent supports `session/fork`.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the agent does not advertise support.
Supplying `{}` means the agent supports forking sessions.

resume.description:
Whether the agent supports `session/resume`.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the agent does not advertise support.
Supplying `{}` means the agent supports resuming sessions.

close.description:
Whether the agent supports `session/close`.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the agent does not advertise support.
Supplying `{}` means the agent supports closing sessions.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionCapabilities.description:
Session capabilities supported by the agent.

As a baseline, all Agents **MUST** support `session/new`, `session/prompt`, `session/cancel`, and `session/update`.

Optionally, they **MAY** support other session methods and notifications by specifying additional capabilities.

Note: `session/load` is still handled by the top-level `load_session` capability. This will be unified in future versions of the protocol.

See protocol docs: [Session Capabilities](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/initialization#session-capabilities)

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionListCapabilities.description:
Capabilities for the `session/list` method.

Supplying `{}` means the agent supports listing sessions.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionDeleteCapabilities.description:
Capabilities for the `session/delete` method.

Supplying `{}` means the agent supports deleting sessions from `session/list`.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionAdditionalDirectoriesCapabilities.description:
Capabilities for additional session directories support.

Supplying `{}` means the agent supports the `additionalDirectories` field on
supported session lifecycle requests. Agents that also support
`session/list` may return `SessionInfo.additionalDirectories` to report the
complete ordered additional-root list associated with a listed session.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionForkCapabilities.description:
**UNSTABLE**

This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.

Capabilities for the `session/fork` method.

Supplying `{}` means the agent supports forking sessions.

Optional. Omitted or `null` both mean the agent does not advertise support.

AgentAuthCapabilities.description: Authentication-related capabilities supported by the agent.

The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional

LogoutCapabilities.description:
Logout capabilities supported by the agent.

Supplying `{}` means the agent supports the logout method.

anyOf.description:
Agent handles authentication itself.

This is the default when no `type` is specified.

AuthMethod.description:
Describes an available authentication method.

The `type` field acts as the discriminator in the serialized JSON form.
When no `type` is present, the method is treated as `agent`.

id.description: Unique identifier for this authentication method.

name.description: Human-readable name of the authentication method.

description.description: Optional description providing more details about this authentication method.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

AuthMethodAgent.description:
Agent handles authentication itself.

This is the default authentication method type.

AuthMethodId.description: Typed identifier used for auth method values on the wire.

methodId.description:
The ID of the authentication method to use.
Must be one of the methods advertised in the initialize response.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional

Specifies which authentication method to use.

Terminates the current authenticated session.

The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

sessionId.description:
Unique identifier for the created session.

Used in all subsequent requests for this conversation.

modes.description:
Initial mode state if supported by the Agent

See protocol docs: [Session Modes](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-modes)

configOptions.description: Initial session configuration options if supported by the Agent.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

NewSessionResponse.description:
Response from creating a new session.

See protocol docs: [Creating a Session](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-setup#creating-a-session)

SessionId.description:
A unique identifier for a conversation session between a client and agent.

Sessions maintain their own context, conversation history, and state,
allowing multiple independent interactions with the same agent.

See protocol docs: [Session ID](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-setup#session-id)

currentModeId.description: The current mode the Agent is in.

availableModes.description: The set of modes that the Agent can operate in

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionModeState.description: The set of modes and the one currently active.

SessionModeId.description: Unique identifier for a Session Mode.

id.description: Stable identifier used to refer to this protocol object in later messages.

name.description: Human-readable name shown for this protocol object.

description.description: Optional human-readable details shown with this protocol object.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

SessionMode.description:
A mode the agent can operate in.

See protocol docs: [Session Modes](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-modes)

id.description: Unique identifier for the configuration option.

description.description: Optional description for the Client to display to the user.

category.description: Optional semantic category for this option (UX only).

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

oneOf.description: Single-value selector (dropdown).
Boolean on/off toggle.

SessionConfigOption.description: A session configuration option selector and its current state.

SessionConfigId.description: Unique identifier for a session configuration option.

SessionConfigOptionCategory.description:
Semantic category for a session configuration option.

This is intended to help Clients distinguish broadly common selectors (e.g. model selector vs
session mode selector vs thought/reasoning level) for UX purposes (keyboard shortcuts, icons,
placement). It MUST NOT be required for correctness. Clients MUST handle missing or unknown
categories gracefully.

Category names beginning with `_` are free for custom use, like other ACP extension methods.
Category names that do not begin with `_` are reserved for the ACP spec.

SessionConfigValueId.description: Unique identifier for a session configuration option value.

anyOf.description: A flat list of options with no grouping.
A list of options grouped under headers.

SessionConfigSelectOptions.description: Possible values for a session configuration option.

value.description: Unique identifier for this option value.

name.description: Human-readable label for this option value.

description.description: Optional description for this option value.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionConfigSelectOption.description: A possible value for a session configuration option.

options.description: The set of option values in this group.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

SessionConfigSelectGroup.description: A group of possible values for a session configuration option.

SessionConfigGroupId.description: Unique identifier for a session configuration option value group.

SessionConfigSelect.description: A single-value selector (dropdown) session configuration option payload.

currentValue.description: The current value of the boolean option.

SessionConfigBoolean.description: A boolean on/off toggle session configuration option payload.

sessionId.description: The ID of the session to send this user message to

prompt.description:
The blocks of content that compose the user's message.

As a baseline, the Agent MUST support [`ContentBlock::Text`] and [`ContentBlock::ResourceLink`],
while other variants are optionally enabled via [`PromptCapabilities`].

The Client MUST adapt its interface according to [`PromptCapabilities`].

The client MAY include referenced pieces of context as either
[`ContentBlock::Resource`] or [`ContentBlock::ResourceLink`].

When available, [`ContentBlock::Resource`] is preferred
as it avoids extra round-trips and allows the message to include
pieces of context from sources the agent may not have access to.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

PromptRequest.description:
Request parameters for sending a user prompt to the agent.

Contains the user's message and any additional context.

See protocol docs: [User Message](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn#1-user-message)

oneOf.description:
Text content. May be plain text or formatted with Markdown.

All agents MUST support text content blocks in prompts.
Clients SHOULD render this text as Markdown.

Images for visual context or analysis.

Requires the `image` prompt capability when included in prompts.

Audio data for transcription or analysis.

Requires the `audio` prompt capability when included in prompts.

References to resources that the agent can access.

All agents MUST support resource links in prompts.

Complete resource contents embedded directly in the message.

Preferred for including context as it avoids extra round-trips.

Requires the `embeddedContext` prompt capability when included in prompts.

ContentBlock.description:
Content blocks represent displayable information in the Agent Client Protocol.

They provide a structured way to handle various types of user-facing content—whether
it's text from language models, images for analysis, or embedded resources for context.

Content blocks appear in:
- User prompts sent via `session/prompt`
- Language model output streamed through `session/update` notifications
- Progress updates and results from tool calls

This structure is compatible with the Model Context Protocol (MCP), enabling
agents to seamlessly forward content from MCP tool outputs without transformation.

See protocol docs: [Content](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/content)

audience.description: Intended recipients for this content, such as the user or assistant.

lastModified.description: Timestamp indicating when the underlying resource was last modified.

priority.description: Relative importance of this content when clients choose what to surface.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

Annotations.description: Optional annotations for the client. The client can use annotations to inform how objects are used or displayed

oneOf.description: The assistant side of a conversation.
The user side of a conversation.

Role.description: The sender or recipient of messages and data in a conversation.

annotations.description: Optional annotations that help clients decide how to display or route this content.

text.description: Text payload carried by this content block.

_meta.description:

PromptResponse.description:
Response from processing a user prompt.

See protocol docs: [Check for Completion](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn#4-check-for-completion)

oneOf.description: The turn ended successfully.
The turn ended because the agent reached the maximum number of tokens.

The turn ended because the agent reached the maximum number of allowed
agent requests between user turns.

The turn ended because the agent refused to continue. The user prompt
and everything that comes after it won't be included in the next
prompt, so this should be reflected in the UI.

The turn was cancelled by the client via `session/cancel`.

This stop reason MUST be returned when the client sends a `session/cancel`
notification, even if the cancellation causes exceptions in underlying operations.
Agents should catch these exceptions and return this semantically meaningful
response to confirm successful cancellation.

StopReason.description:
Reasons why an agent stops processing a prompt turn.

See protocol docs: [Stop Reasons](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn#stop-reasons)

sessionId.description: The ID of the session to resume.

cwd.description: The working directory for this session. Must be an absolute path.

additionalDirectories.description:
Additional workspace roots to activate for this session. Each path must be absolute.

When omitted or empty, no additional roots are activated. When non-empty,
this is the complete resulting additional-root list for the resumed
session. It may differ from any previously used or reported list as long as
the request `cwd` matches the session's `cwd`.

mcpServers.description: List of MCP servers to connect to for this session.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at
these keys.

See protocol docs: [Extensibility](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/extensibility)

ResumeSessionRequest.description:
Request parameters for resuming an existing session.

Resumes an existing session without returning previous messages (unlike `session/load`).
This is useful for agents that can resume sessions but don't implement full session loading.

Only available if the Agent supports the `sessionCapabilities.resume` capability.

anyOf.description:
HTTP transport configuration

Only available when the Agent capabilities indicate `mcp_capabilities.http` is `true`.

SSE transport configuration

Only available when the Agent capabilities indicate `mcp_capabilities.sse` is `true`.

Stdio transport configuration

All Agents MUST support this transport.

McpServer.description:
Configuration for connecting to an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server.

MCP servers provide tools and context that the agent can use when
processing prompts.

See protocol docs: [MCP Servers](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/session-setup#mcp-servers)

Request parameters for closing an active session.

If supported, the agent **must** cancel any ongoing work related to the session
(treat it as if `session/cancel` was called) and then free up any resources
associated with the session.

Only available if the Agent supports the `sessionCapabilities.close` capability.

ForkSessionRequest.description:
**UNSTABLE**

This capability is not part of the spec yet, and may be removed or changed at any point.

Request parameters for forking an existing session.

Creates a new session based on the context of an existing one, allowing
operations like generating summaries without affecting the original session's history.

Only available if the Agent supports the `session.fork` capability.

sessionId.description: Unique identifier for the newly created forked session.

reports no additional roots.

RequestPermissionRequest.description:
Request for user permission to execute a tool call.

Sent when the agent needs authorization before performing a sensitive operation.

See protocol docs: [Requesting Permission](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#requesting-permission)

toolCallId.description: The ID of the tool call being updated.

_meta.description:
The _meta property is reserved by ACP to allow clients and agents to attach additional
metadata to their interactions. Implementations MUST NOT make assumptions about values at

ToolCallUpdate.description:
An update to an existing tool call.

Used to report progress and results as tools execute. All fields except
the tool call ID are optional - only changed fields need to be included.

See protocol docs: [Updating](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#updating)

ToolCallId.description: Unique identifier for a tool call within a session.

oneOf.description: Reading files or data.
Modifying files or content.
Removing files or data.
Moving or renaming files.
Searching for information.
Running commands or code.
Internal reasoning or planning.
Retrieving external data.
Switching the current session mode.
Other tool types (default).

ToolKind.description:
Categories of tools that can be invoked.

Tool kinds help clients choose appropriate icons and optimize how they
display tool execution progress.

See protocol docs: [Creating](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#creating)

oneOf.description:
The tool call hasn't started running yet because the input is either
streaming or we're awaiting approval.
The tool call is currently running.
The tool call completed successfully.
The tool call failed with an error.

ToolCallStatus.description:
Execution status of a tool call.

Tool calls progress through different statuses during their lifecycle.

See protocol docs: [Status](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#status)

oneOf.description: Standard content block (text, images, resources).
File modification shown as a diff.

Embed a terminal created with `terminal/create` by its id.

The terminal must be added before calling `terminal/release`.

See protocol docs: [Terminal](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/terminals)

ToolCallContent.description:
Content produced by a tool call.

Tool calls can produce different types of content including
standard content blocks (text, images) or file diffs.

See protocol docs: [Content](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/tool-calls#content)

_meta.description:

prompt turn, it MUST respond to all pending `session/request_permission`
requests with this `Cancelled` outcome.

See protocol docs: [Cancellation](https://agentclientprotocol.com/protocol/prompt-turn#cancellation)
The user selected one of the provided options.

outputByteLimit.description:
Maximum number of output bytes to retain.

When the limit is exceeded, the Client truncates from the beginning of the output
to stay within the limit.

The Client MUST ensure truncation happens at a character boundary to maintain valid
string output, even if this means the retained output is slightly less than the
specified limit.

WaitForTerminalExitRequest.description: Request to wait for a terminal command to exit.
